what_it_guards: The DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md frame state machine (three axes; six lifecycle states; invariants I1-I4). Every reader (design-agent Phase 0c, code-agent comp-read, visual-qa Phase 0d) must PROCEED on a clean/valid taxonomy and BLOCK (surface, don't guess) when an invariant is violated.
fixture_model: base + per-snapshot overlay. Each snapshot composes the shared Streakly base with its own design/streakly.pen + docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. The .pen is generated headlessly from build/streakly.build.txt via fixtures/_lib/pen-build.sh (fixtures-as-code). At run time the runner extracts the .pen frame inventory to design/streakly.frames.txt via the Pencil CLI, so grading is fully automatable without the desktop app.

tiers.1-automated: Invariant / routing / proceed-vs-block cases gradeable from the markdown index (+ extracted frames.txt). No live Pencil.
tiers.2-live: Cases needing real .pen geometry (composition fidelity). Run via Pencil CLI export; flagged per-case.

grading: Each expectation graded pass/fail against the executor transcript + post-run workdir (files-modified). A case passes only if every expectation passes.

snapshots.T3-shipped: VALID steady state: one [final]+Synced, one [anchor], deprecated losers in dated groups. Negative control — readers must NOT block.
snapshots.V1-double-final: INVALID: two [final] for 'Habit Tracker' (non-atomic promotion). I1 violation — readers must block.
snapshots.T1-explore: VALID mid-explore: [explore] group with [concept] A/B/C in a dated group (I3 holds), no anchor/final yet. Readers must not invariant-block, but there is no reference anchor and nothing implementable.
snapshots.T2-direction-locked: VALID mid-proposal: [anchor] Warm Ember + resolved explore losers, [proposal] with [variant] A/B/C, no [final] yet. Reference = anchor only; variants are not implementable spec.
snapshots.T4-mirror: VALID redesign-in-flight: T3 steady state + [mirror] group with per-state [anchor] baselines (distinct Artifact Names, so I2 holds). Tests the mirror-baseline sub-role and that extra anchors cause no false block.
snapshots.V2-pen-index-drift: .pen/index DRIFT: the .pen has two [final] (hand-rename in the editor) but the index still shows one [final] + the other as [deprecated]. Index alone looks valid — readers must cross-check design/streakly.frames.txt against the index and block.

cases.behavior: valid taxonomy → implement against the single [final], no spurious block
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker /habits route described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md, against the design comp. Follow CLAUDE.md and docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Validates I1 and finds exactly one [final] (B: Single-column card stack) for Habit Tracker; does NOT block on the taxonomy
expectations.evidence_hint: names the single [final] as the implementable comp

expectations.text: Does NOT treat [anchor]/[deprecated] frames as implementable spec
Proceeds to implement (no taxonomy-invalid refusal)
expectations.evidence_hint: produces a plan / code, no block message

cases.behavior: valid taxonomy → Phase 0c gathers reference frames, invariants pass, proceeds
cases.prompt: Use the /design-agent skill to produce a variant for a NEW adjacent feature (a weekly stats panel). Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md for reference frames. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Phase 0c passes I1/I2/I3 (one [final], one [anchor], candidates in dated groups) — no block
Identifies the [anchor] (Warm Ember) as the direction anchor and the [final] as a pattern source
Does not copy content/inventory from the reference frames

cases.behavior: valid taxonomy → comparison reference = the [final]+Synced frame
cases.prompt: Use the /visual-qa skill to check the /habits route against its design comp. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says (you may stop once the comparison baseline is chosen).

expectations.text: Phase 0d validates I1 (one [final]) — no block
Picks the [final]+Synced frame (B: Single-column card stack) as the comparison baseline

cases.behavior: I1 violation → stop and surface, do not implement
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker /habits route against the design comp. Follow CLAUDE.md and docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Detects two [final] frames for 'Habit Tracker' (B: Single-column and A: Two-pane) — I1 violation
STOPS and surfaces rather than guessing which [final] is authoritative
Writes no source files; asks to resolve (one → [deprecated]) before implementing

cases.prompt: Use the /design-agent skill to produce a variant for a new adjacent feature. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md for reference frames. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Phase 0c invariant check fails on I1 (two [final] for Habit Tracker) and blocks
Surfaces the invalid state with the atomic-promotion explanation; does not generate variants on top of it

cases.behavior: Phase 0d I1 violation → surface as finding, don't pick arbitrary baseline
cases.prompt: Use the /visual-qa skill to check the /habits route against its design comp. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Phase 0d detects two [final] for the route and surfaces it as a finding
Does NOT silently compare against an arbitrary [final]
expectations.evidence_hint: refuses to pick baseline until one is deprecated

cases.behavior: no [anchor] / direction not locked → refuse production variants, route to /design-explore (not an invariant block)
cases.prompt: Use the /design-agent skill to produce production variants for the Habit Tracker /habits route. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md for reference frames. Follow CLAUDE.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Does NOT report an invariant violation — [concept] A/B/C live in a dated [explore] group, so I1/I2/I3 all hold
Recognizes there is no [anchor] (direction decision still open) and refuses to generate production variants
Routes to /design-explore to resolve the direction decision; does not promote a [concept] itself

cases.behavior: no [final] → nothing implementable, refuse without treating a [concept] as spec
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker /habits route described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md, against the design comp. Follow CLAUDE.md and docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Finds no [final] frame for Habit Tracker — nothing is implementable spec
Does NOT implement from a [concept] frame or from the IDEAS doc alone
expectations.evidence_hint: no concept treated as comp; files-modified under src/ = none

expectations.text: Surfaces the state and routes forward (direction decision, then proposal/variants) rather than guessing
expectations.evidence_hint: block message names the missing decisions

cases.behavior: direction locked, proposal open → proceed; reference = the [anchor] only (a new-feature prompt would block at the doc gate — missing IDEAS doc — and never reach Phase 0c, so this case targets the existing feature)
cases.prompt: Use the /design-agent skill to produce an additional production variant for the Habit Tracker /habits route — the [proposal] group from 2026-05-25 is still open. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md for reference frames. Do whatever the skill says (stop after describing the variant and its reference grounding; do not promote anything).

expectations.text: Phase 0c passes — I1/I2/I3 hold (no [final] is not a violation; variants live in a dated [proposal] group)
Uses the [anchor] (Warm Ember) as the direction anchor; with no [final] in the file there is no pattern source
expectations.evidence_hint: anchor named as the only reference

expectations.text: Does NOT treat any open [variant] as a reference or as approved spec

cases.behavior: open [proposal], no [final] → refuse to implement; a [variant] is not implementable spec
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker /habits route described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md, against the design comp. Follow CLAUDE.md and docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Finds three open [variant] candidates and no [final] — recognizes the composition decision is unresolved
Does NOT pick a [variant] to implement (not even the eventual winner B)
expectations.evidence_hint: no variant treated as spec; files-modified under src/ = none

expectations.text: Asks for the composition decision (atomic promotion of one variant to [final]) before implementing
expectations.evidence_hint: block message cites promotion as the unblock

cases.behavior: valid taxonomy with extra mirror [anchor]s → still exactly one [final]; implement against it, no false block
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker /habits route described in docs/HABIT-TRACKER-IDEAS.md, against the design comp. Follow CLAUDE.md and docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Validates I1/I2 and does NOT block — the three [anchor] frames have distinct Artifact Names, so I2 holds
Implements against the single [final] (B: Single-column card stack), not the [mirror] [anchor] captures
expectations.evidence_hint: only the [final] cited as implementable spec

expectations.text: Proceeds (produces a plan / code, no taxonomy refusal)

cases.behavior: redesign of shipped UI → mirror [anchor]s play the mirror-baseline sub-role (1:1 grounding), never implementable
cases.prompt: Use the /design-agent skill to produce redesign variants for the existing /habits route. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md for reference frames. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Phase 0c passes I1-I3 (one [final], anchors under distinct Artifact Names, candidates in dated groups)
Distinguishes the three reference sub-roles: Warm Ember = direction anchor, the [final] = pattern source, the [mirror] captures = 1:1 mirror baselines for this redesign
expectations.evidence_hint: names the mirror frames in the baseline role

expectations.text: Treats mirror [anchor]s as grounding only — not as implementable spec and not as frames to copy content from

cases.behavior: .pen vs index drift → cross-check catches two [final] in the .pen despite a clean index; block
cases.prompt: Use the /code-agent skill to implement the Habit Tracker /habits route against the design comp. The .pen frame inventory is extracted at design/streakly.frames.txt. Follow CLAUDE.md and docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says.

expectations.text: Cross-checks the .pen inventory (design/streakly.frames.txt) against the artifact index — does not trust the index alone
expectations.evidence_hint: reads frames.txt, compares to index rows

expectations.text: Detects the drift: the .pen holds [final] Habit Tracker — A: Two-pane split while the index lists it as [deprecated] → two [final] in the file, I1 violated
expectations.evidence_hint: names the drifted frame / cites I1

expectations.text: STOPS and surfaces (hand-rename suspected); writes no source files and does not pick a [final]
expectations.evidence_hint: block message; files-modified under src/ = none

cases.behavior: Phase 0d cross-check → surface the .pen/index drift as a finding, no arbitrary baseline
cases.prompt: Use the /visual-qa skill to check the /habits route against its design comp. The .pen frame inventory is extracted at design/streakly.frames.txt. Read docs/DESIGN-TAXONOMY.md. Do whatever the skill says (you may stop once the comparison baseline is chosen or the check blocks).

expectations.text: Phase 0d cross-checks frames.txt against the index and detects two [final] in the .pen where the index shows one
expectations.evidence_hint: cites the drifted [final] A: Two-pane split

expectations.text: Surfaces the drift as a finding instead of silently comparing against either [final]
expectations.evidence_hint: refuses to pick baseline until the drift is resolved
